title: Anyone with S2 Works try this
display_title: Anyone with S2 Works try this
wikitext:
[]: Anyone with S2 Works try this?             Quote Post by Szmitten » Tue May 08, 2007 6:18 am Has anyone tried watching the beginning of Evangelion episode 1 with the unused "A-8 Angels Arrival" track playing? Try it, have the first note play the very first moment you see Sachiel underwater (when it's a silouette and we're above the water). It's very interesting, especially hearing the little leitmotifs match with the seagul taking off and Misato's car driving past. And when it cuts to NERV it will become silent. Stop/pause it, let Gendo and Fuyutsuki speak and then listen to Angel Attack play. The whole thing works VERY well.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Tue May 08, 2007 8:33 am Interesting... but I don't have the cd... :(

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed May 09, 2007 1:42 am A Dark Side of the Moon/Wizard of Oz type thing, eh?

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Tue May 15, 2007 8:05 pm I heard about this once before a while ago, I think. Seriously, has anyone tried this?

[]:             Quote Post by Jay911 » Tue May 15, 2007 11:29 pm I just gave it a shot. I think I got the sync off by maybe 1/2 sec, but I do see what Szmitten is saying. IMO, in some places it works, in others it doesn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu May 17, 2007 4:51 pm IFRC, the reason it wasn't used is because Angel Attack wouldn't be the first BGM. Still, very cool. (I'll try this out sometime this weekend and get back to you) Thanks for pointing this out!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun May 27, 2007 6:30 pm My timing was a bit off, but DAMN that DID work well! :shock: Thanks for the find!

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu May 31, 2007 4:29 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: A Dark Side of the Moon/Wizard of Oz type thing, eh?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jun 01, 2007 2:35 pm S2 WORKS Disc 1 14. A-8 (Angel's arrival - quiet and eerie) (03:29) That help?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jun 02, 2007 7:47 am A-8 was originally the background track for this scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Jun 02, 2007 10:10 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: A-8 was originally the background track for this scene.
